You've got the quick export, which is this option in the top right. So, if you select quick export, you can select a file name and location. You can select a preset. So, for example, match source, adaptive high bit rate, or you can add a custom setting, and you just press export. Alternatively though, I like a little bit more control on the export. So, I'm going to go up to file, export media, and this brings up the export media window. Now, as you can see, this is the video clip that we have created. So, this was our speed ramping effect that we created. We've got our transitions happening here. This is our flash and this is our slide transition. chunks.text: Multi-cam editing is a really quick and efficient way of cutting down your videos if you've got multiple takes of the same thing. So, for example, if you have filmed an interview, if you filmed a show, if you filmed a music video, using the multi-cam editor is a really quick and easy way to cut through all of your individual clips and get to a final result much quicker. And I'm going to show you how to do that right now. So, as you can see on my timeline, I have got four clips. Now, these are from a music video. These are different performance shots. And rather than stacking them all on top of each other and picking my favorite bits, I can do an initial first edit by using the multicam editor. So to do that, I'm just going to stack every individual clip onto a different layer. So this is the first clip. So video one, audio one. There we go. They're stacked on top of each other like this. Now video two and audio two can go onto a separate channel. This third clip, this can go three and three. And this clip here can go four and four. Now, from here, we're just going to use the autos synchronization feature to highlight all of these and sync them together. So, highlight everything, right click, synchronize. We're going to go audio track channel. So, the autos synchronization feature is essentially going to listen to the audio on every single track and find points that are in sync and just basically marry those up with each other. If it does fail though, then you can just manually sync these in by listening to certain sync points. But the autosync feature is definitely a game changer. But once that has done that, you can highlight all your clips. We're going to rightclick and we're going to go nest. So, we'll select nest and we'll put this into its own nested sequence. So we'll go music video nest. Press okay. And this is now in its own nested sequence. Now from here we can rightclick go multi camera and enable. Now we'll go into this setting here. And we're going to change the view from a composite video into multi camera. And as you can see we've now got four different video clips playing on our timeline. So, if we play this back, you can see these are four different
